name: Yamalo-Nenets Autonomous Okrug (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the snowy expanses, sky and waters of the Arctic
the snow and the traditional bone-and-antler ornaments of the Nenets people
the courage of the peoples of the okrug; the thin red stripe near the bottom

description: A light-blue field bearing a stylised white Nenets reindeer-antler ornament running diagonally from the lower hoist to the upper fly, and a thin red horizontal stripe near the bottom of the flag.
history: Adopted on 28 December 1996 by the Law of the Yamalo-Nenets Autonomous Okrug; designed by Vladimir Stepanchenko, the flag draws on the visual heritage of the Nenets and other indigenous peoples of Yamal.

features.symbolism: A light-blue field charged with a white reindeer-antler ornament running diagonally across the lower hoist, with a thin red horizontal line near the bottom
the traditional Nenets ornament known as 'rogi olenya' (reindeer horns), running diagonally across the lower part of the flag, evoking the herding traditions of Yamal's indigenous peoples
